mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%

source_info.section: **Association between serum phosphate and mortality in critically ill patients: a large retrospective cohort study**

population: Critically ill patients admitted to ICU (MIMIC-III database)
predictor: Serum phosphate at admission (hypophosphataemia and hyperphosphataemia vs. normal reference group ≥2.5 mg/dL and <3.0 mg/dL)
standardized_predictor: Serum phosphate at admission (hypophosphataemia and hyperphosphataemia vs. normal reference group ≥2.5 mg/dL and <3.0 mg/dL)
method: Cox proportional hazards regression with adjustment for potential confounders
effect_size: Hypophosphataemia and hyperphosphataemia: not significantly associated; Medical ICU hyperphosphataemia: HR=0.64, 95% CI 0.48 to 0.84
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%
notes: Patients divided into six serum phosphate groups; reference group was ≥2.5 mg/dL and <3.0 mg/dL. Linear relationship observed between serum phosphate and probability of 28-day or 90-day mortality. Very-high-normal serum phosphate also associated with worse outcomes.

source_info.section: **ABSTRACT**

population: ICU patients from MIMIC database, age ≥18 years, with serum phosphate measured within first day of ICU admission, no phosphate supplementation during ICU stay
predictor: Admission serum phosphate level (grouped: G1 <2.5 mg/dL; G2 ≥2.5 to <3.0 mg/dL; G3 ≥3.0 to <3.5 mg/dL; G4 ≥3.5 to <4.0 mg/dL; G5 ≥4.0 to <4.5 mg/dL; G6 ≥4.5 mg/dL)
standardized_predictor: Admission serum phosphate level (grouped: G1 <2.5 mg/dL; G2 ≥2.5 to <3.0 mg/dL; G3 ≥3.0 to <3.5 mg/dL; G4 ≥3.5 to <4.0 mg/dL; G5 ≥4.0 to <4.5 mg/dL; G6 ≥4.5 mg/dL)
timing: Within first day of ICU admission
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%
notes: Study describes patient grouping methodology based on serum phosphate concentration gradients of 0.5 mg/dL intervals from 2.5 mg/dL. No outcome data or statistical results are reported in this excerpt.

source_info.section: **Selection of participants and stratification method**

population: ICU patients (sepsis cohort from MIMIC database)
predictor: Serum phosphate level at ICU admission
standardized_predictor: Serum phosphate level at ICU admission
outcome: All-cause 28-day and 90-day mortality after ICU admission
standardized_outcome: All-cause 28-day and 90-day mortality after ICU admission
method: Kaplan-Meier survival analysis, restricted cubic spline, univariate and multivariable Cox regression (Model 1 and Model 2)
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%
notes: Model 1 adjusted for age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation. Model 2 additionally adjusted for admission types, ICU types, SOFA score, SAPS II, OASIS score, congestive heart failure, coronary artery disease, hypertension, diabetes, chronic pulmonary disease, atrial fibrillation, liver disease, fluid and electrolyte disorder, malignant tumour, chronic kidney disease, serum sodium, serum potassium, serum calcium, serum creatinine, blood urea nitrogen, serum bicarbonate, serum chloride, white cell count, haemoglobin, platelet, use of mechanical ventilation. Actual results not reported in this excerpt.

source_info.section: **Statistical analysis**

population: ICU patients with sepsis enrolled from MIMIC database, stratified into 6 phosphate groups
effect_size: CKD prevalence 28.82%; serum creatinine 2.66±2.50; BUN 43.65±31.06; mechanical ventilation 44.27%; renal replacement therapy 1.89%; vasopressor 32.96%
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%
notes: G1 n=3060 (11.28%), G2 n=4257 (15.69%), G3 n=5804 (21.39%), G4 n=5395 (19.89%), G5 n=3584 (13.21%), G6 n=5031 (18.54%). Mean age 64.00±17.94 years; 55.60% male; 71.46% white; 85.61% emergency admission. G6 had highest disease severity and resource utilization among all groups.

source_info.section: **Associations between serum phosphate and clinical outcomes**

population: General critically ill ICU patients (subset analysis by sex)
predictor: Hypophosphataemia (<2.5 mg/dL) and hyperphosphataemia (≥4.5 mg/dL) at ICU admission
standardized_predictor: Hypophosphataemia (<2.5 mg/dL) and hyperphosphataemia (≥4.5 mg/dL) at ICU admission
method: Multivariable Cox regression (model 2), subset analyses by sex
performance: p=0.0160 (referenced for a specific comparison); not significant for hypophosphataemia and hyperphosphataemia as independent risk factors
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%
notes: 95% CI 0.42 to 0.91, p=0.0160 referenced in context of adjusted model 2 for a specific comparison. Hypophosphataemia and hyperphosphataemia at ICU admission were not independent risk factors for 28-day or 90-day mortality in general critically ill patients in sex-stratified analyses.

source_info.section: **Associations between serum phosphate and clinical outcomes**

population: General critically ill ICU patients, female subgroup
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%

source_info.section: **Associations between serum phosphate and clinical outcomes**

population: General critically ill ICU patients with serum phosphate measurements within the first day of ICU admission
predictor: Serum phosphate at ICU admission (six groups: G1 hypophosphataemia <2.5 mg/dL; G2 ≥2.5 and <3.0 mg/dL; G3 ≥3.0 and <3.5 mg/dL; G4 ≥3.5 and <4.0 mg/dL; G5 ≥4.0 and <4.5 mg/dL; G6 hyperphosphataemia ≥4.5 mg/dL)
standardized_predictor: Serum phosphate at ICU admission (six groups: G1 hypophosphataemia <2.5 mg/dL; G2 ≥2.5 and <3.0 mg/dL; G3 ≥3.0 and <3.5 mg/dL; G4 ≥3.5 and <4.0 mg/dL; G5 ≥4.0 and <4.5 mg/dL; G6 hyperphosphataemia ≥4.5 mg/dL)
timing: Within the first day of ICU admission
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%
notes: Hypophosphataemia and hyperphosphataemia at ICU admission were not independent risk factors of 28- or 90-day mortality for general critically ill patients per the authors' discussion of prior literature; Kaplan-Meier curves showed significant difference in survival distributions across six phosphate groups.

source_info.section: **Associations between serum phosphate and clinical outcomes**

population: Critically ill patients in ICU (MIMIC-III database)
method: Multivariable Cox regression analysis; cubic spline analysis
performance: Clinical outcomes ranked approximately G6 > G5 > G1 > G2, G3, G4
mechanical_ventilation: G1 34.44%, G2 32.49%, G3 31.96%, G4 33.35%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 44.27%
vasopressor_use: G1 22.25%, G2 18.68%, G3 18.90%, G4 20.20%; overall 22.62%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 32.96%
rrt_use: G1 0.16%, G2 0.12%, G3 0.03%, G4 0.17%; overall 0.47%; hyperphosphataemia (G6) group 1.89%
notes: Very-high-normal phosphate (G5) was associated with 28- or 90-day mortality in critically ill patients non-adjusted or adjusted by model 1 (age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation). Authors suggest possible need to redefine normal range of serum phosphate. A figure (Figure 3) showing cubic spline analysis of serum phosphate as continuous variable vs 28-day and 90-day mortality probability was omitted from extraction.

notes: After stratification by ICU type, hyperphosphataemia at admission was associated with increased mortality risk specifically in MICU patients. Hypophosphataemia and hyperphosphataemia were not independent risk factors for 28 or 90-day mortality in the general critically ill population after correction for confounders.

source_info.section: **CONCLUSION**
